subject: eddy-lazzarin

sectors_excited.name: Zero-knowledge proofs / SNARKs / ZK VMs
sectors_excited.why: Decades-old theory has crossed into practice and is accelerating because of crypto. With Lasso/Jolt the overhead is collapsing, which unlocks both scaling-through-succinctness and privacy. This is the substrate for verifiable compute, content provenance, and a new class of applications. It's literally what I spend my days building.

citations.quote: This is why we're developing ⚡️ Jolt, our open source proof of concept reference implementation free forever public good love letter to crypto ZK VM.
We've developed a new lookup argument called Lasso 🪢 that is already 10x faster than halo2, with a clear path to 40x.

sectors_excited.name: Crypto × AI (provenance, ownership, deepfake defense)
sectors_excited.why: We're months away from non-technical people generating convincing fakes at scale — the 'mystery meat' internet where content is assumed generated unless proven otherwise. Every robust answer (provenance graphs, attestation, sybil-resistant proof of personhood) requires a censorship-resistant verifiable computer. AI is how machines think; crypto is how machines own. The two compound.

citations.quote: Every robust method to prove the origin or history of content involves cryptography and blockchains.
AI + crypto = how machines can think on their own

sectors_excited.name: Identity, auth & wallet UX (proof of personhood, smart accounts, passkeys)
sectors_excited.why: Crypto UX has barely moved since 2016, and that's the binding constraint on mainstream adoption. Smart accounts, embedded wallets, passkeys, MPC, and smarter RPC can make the experience better and more secure than web2. Pair that with a private, decentralized proof of personhood and you get the identity layer the open web has always needed — increasingly urgent as AI floods the network with fake actors.

citations.quote: the fundamentals of user experience in crypto haven't actually changed much since 2016.
How bad does this need to get before all networks need to integrate a private proof of personhood system like @worldnetwork?

sectors_excited.name: Layer-2 scaling / cheap high-quality blockspace
sectors_excited.why: Radically lowering the cost of credibly-neutral blockspace is the single ingredient that unlocks the experimentation needed to find mainstream products — cheap NFTs, on-chain games, graph data structures, complex markets. Next-gen infra is now coming online. This is the enabling substrate beneath almost everything else I'm excited about.

citations.quote: with next gen infra online, regulatory strategies improving, and UX evolving — there has never been a better time to build.
radically decreasing the cost of blocks, high quality blockspace, is a key ingredient in allowing for experimentation

sectors_excited.name: Fully on-chain games
sectors_excited.why: Games push the envelope in every computing wave and generate far more on-chain activity than DeFi. When no one in particular runs the server — state governed by rules set at genesis — modding, composability, and remixing become first-class. As blockspace gets cheap, this niche (the spiritual successor to the Zachtronics-style builder games I love) fills in.
sectors_excited.age_note: stated 2022-06; reaffirmed through 2023 via the on-chain games podcast and playing Primodium

citations.quote: I believe fully on-chain games will fill a similar niche in coming years.

sectors_excited.name: Decentralized social / open networks
sectors_excited.why: Every closed corporate network eventually breaks its promise to creators and developers the moment it's safe to — App Store, Reddit, LinkedIn, the Fediverse degrading to centralization. The answer is networks where names, value, and data live in a permissionless, programmable, unified computer. Farcaster is the clearest live example, and the ecosystem of clients on top is exactly the permissionless innovation we wanted.

citations.quote: Farcaster is the neutral platform we wanted all along.

sectors_concerned.name: Memecoins / casino speculation
sectors_concerned.why: Right now the timeline is captured by memecoin fomo. People who know better spin elaborate rationalizations for what is fundamentally zero-sum, short-term casino behavior. It distracts builders and talent from the real potential, and it's the kind of froth I distrust on principle — skeptical in analysis.

citations.quote: When memecoins have a moment, people who know better make tortured rationalizations.

sectors_concerned.name: Token-first thinking / pie-chart 'tokenomics'
sectors_concerned.why: A token is a tool, not a goal. 'Tokenomics consultants' who hand you an allocation pie chart are doing maybe 5% of the work and calling it the protocol. The right object to design is the protocol — goals, constraints, mechanisms — with the token serving long-term decentralization. The persistence of this consultant class is a drag on serious design.

citations.quote: A made up pie chart is not real work.
Launching a token isn't a goal.

sectors_concerned.name: Bitcoin's long-term security model
sectors_concerned.why: As the block subsidy declines, Bitcoin's security budget is a real multi-decade question that too few take seriously. I think the path forward involves making it programmable and cheap to move — something like a VERIFY_SNARK opcode would revive much of what Satoshi envisioned — but absent change, ossification is a genuine risk to its survival over the next 100 years.

citations.quote: the security model of bitcoin needs to change if it's going to last the next few decades.

tokens_excited.name: ETH
tokens_excited.why: Native asset of the leading smart-contract computer and the ecosystem I build on daily — most developers, most mindshare, and a scaling roadmap (L2s, danksharding, ZK) that's executing. I hold this as a conviction in the network's fundamentals, not a tactical price call.
tokens_excited.age_note: stated 2021-02; long-held Ethereum-as-leading-computer conviction, not a recent price call

citations.quote: In one year, Ethereum has grown from settling ~$373m per day to over $9b per day.

tokens_excited.name: MKR / DAI
tokens_excited.why: My canonical example of token design done right: DAI a decentralized stablecoin native to Ethereum, MKR a stakeholder token that underwrites protocol risk and is diluted on shortfall — real fundamentals and distributed risk/reward, not a pie chart. I cite it as a design exemplar of fundamentals-backed tokens, not as a buy recommendation.
tokens_excited.age_note: stated 2020-12; used as my token-design exemplar through 2023-01, a fundamentals view not a price call

citations.quote: If you look at the top DAOs, most have fundamentals now.

risk_regime.why: I don't time markets — I read them through the price-innovation cycle, where prices are a lagging surface over steady fundamental progress. Skeptical in analysis, optimistic in action: I'm wary of the current memecoin-driven risk appetite, but structurally constructive on building because the infra, regulatory strategies, and UX are all advancing. Net: no tactical risk-on call, but a genuinely good time to build.

citations.quote: there has never been a better time to build.
the right balance is to be skeptical in analysis and optimistic in action.

notes: I'm an engineer/builder, not a trader — these are technology-adoption views, not investment advice (see a16z disclosures). I evaluate tokens as protocol fundamentals, never pie charts. Backdrop risk I'm watching: continued US regulatory uncertainty pushing builders offshore. Vanity metrics like TVL remain a poor lens for any of this.
